name: Medina de las Torres - Valencia del Ventoso - Fuente de Cantos Loop
name_original: Valencia del Ventoso - Fuente de Cantos — circular desde Medina de las Torres
description: This moderate 32.8-mile road cycling route from Medina de las Torres offers scenic countryside views and historical landmarks.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Medina De Las Torres traverse a diverse geographical landscape in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura. The region features rolling terrain, characterized by agricultural lands such as vineyards and olive groves, and connects historical towns. These routes often include moderate elevation changes, providing varied cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Medina De Las Torres The most popular road cycling route is Medina de las Torres - Valencia del Ventoso - Fuente de Cantos Loop, a 32.8 miles (52.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns, offering a scenic journey through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Among vineyards and olive groves of Zafra — return from Medina de las Torres, an easy 22.0 miles (35.4 km) path. This route leads through extensive vineyards and olive groves, characteristic of the region, and passes by the historic town of Zafra. Local road cyclists also love the Zafra Old Town – Puebla de Sancho Pérez loop from Medina de las Torres, a 26.6 miles (42.9 km) trail leading through Zafra Old Town, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. This moderate route combines historical landmarks with rural landscapes. Road cycling around Medina De Las Torres is defined by rolling hills, agricultural landscapes with vineyards and olive groves, and connections between historical towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Medina De Las Torres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from several reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Medina De Las Torres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Medina De Las Torres?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Medina De Las Torres, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Medina De Las Torres?
A: The region features diverse terrain, characterized by rolling hills and agricultural landscapes, including extensive vineyards and olive groves. Routes often connect historical towns and offer moderate elevation changes, providing varied cycling experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Medina De Las Torres?
A: Medina De Las Torres, located in Extremadura, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, ideal for longer rides through the region's diverse landscapes. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Medina De Las Torres offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992931302/among-vineyards-and-olive-groves-of-zafra-return-from-medina-de-las-torres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Among vineyards and olive groves of Zafra — return from Medina de las Torres&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22-mile path that takes you through characteristic regional landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 22 difficult routes available. These routes often feature more significant elevation gains and longer distances, providing a robust workout while exploring the region.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes in Medina De Las Torres?
A: Road cycling routes in the area can lead you past several historical and cultural sites. You might encounter the ancient Roman city of Contributa Iulia, or in nearby Zafra, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parador Nacional Duques de Feria&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3804712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Tower of Zafra&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3757981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Our Lady of Granada&lt;/a&gt; in Fuente de Cantos is another notable stop.

Q: Are there loop road cycling routes available in Medina De Las Torres?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Medina De Las Torres are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995565935/medina-de-las-torres-valencia-del-ventoso-fuente-de-cantos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina de las Torres - Valencia del Ventoso - Fuente de Cantos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 32.8-mile ride.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Medina De Las Torres?
A: While specific dedicated cyclist parking facilities are not detailed, Medina De Las Torres, as a municipality promoting cycle tourism, generally offers public parking options in and around the town center where you can safely park your vehicle before starting your ride.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes using public transport in Medina De Las Torres?
A: Medina De Las Torres has local public transport connections. However, the feasibility of transporting bicycles on public transport can vary. It's advisable to check with local bus operators for their specific policies regarding bicycle carriage if you plan to use public transport to access routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: For families, the easier, flatter sections of routes, particularly those passing through agricultural lands, can be suitable. The region's emphasis on cycle tourism suggests a general bike-friendly environment, but always check route difficulty and length to match your family's capabilities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992931302/among-vineyards-and-olive-groves-of-zafra-return-from-medina-de-las-torres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Among vineyards and olive groves of Zafra — return from Medina de las Torres&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy option to consider.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Medina De Las Torres?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse geographical landscape, the scenic journeys through agricultural lands like vineyards and olive groves, and the connections between historical towns, offering a rich cultural and natural experience.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through Zafra Old Town?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafra Old Town – Puebla de Sancho Pérez loop from Medina de las Torres&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.6-mile route that specifically leads through Zafra Old Town, combining historical exploration with rural landscapes.
tips: Valencia del Ventoso is a town located near Zafra on the southern edge of the Zafrense area, already in the foothills of the southern sierras, on land covered with meadows and scrubland, of outstanding natural beauty.
Fuente de Cantos is a municipality in the province of Badajoz. With Arab heritage, in the same region there was an Arab town that has now disappeared, called Lakant, on the left bank of the Guadiana.
The main altarpiece of the parish church is one of the few Gothic examples that are preserved in Extremadura.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is approximately 52.9 kilometers (32.9 miles) long. Cyclists can expect to complete it in roughly 2 hours and 24 minutes, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is described as a circular tour from Medina de las Torres, suggesting this is the primary starting and ending point. While specific parking details aren't provided, Medina de las Torres would be the logical place to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily a road cycling route, meaning you can expect mostly paved surfaces suitable for a racebike. However, some sections may be unpaved and potentially challenging, requiring a good level of fitness. The route includes an elevation gain of around 550 meters (1,804 feet).

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as moderate and requires a good level of fitness. While it's a road cycling route, the distance and elevation gain make it more suitable for experienced cyclists rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The broader region of Valencia and Extremadura generally offers good cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, providing milder temperatures and pleasant scenery for road cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You can see the Castle of the Order of Santiago in Valencia del Ventoso, the settlement of Fuente de Cantos, and the Church of San Juan Bautista and Mudéjar Gothic Altarpiece.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists due to safety concerns with traffic and the pace of racebiking. If you plan to bring a dog, it would need to be in a specialized trailer, but this route is not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the traditional sense for walking or running alongside.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. It is freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through settlements like Fuente de Cantos and starts from Medina de las Torres. These towns are likely to have cafes, pubs, or shops where you can refuel and find refreshments.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect scenic views of the Spanish countryside. The region is known for rolling plains, traditional landscapes, and potentially cork oak forests, offering a picturesque backdrop for your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a racebike tour of this length and difficulty?
faqs.answer: For a moderate 52.9 km racebike tour, you should bring adequate water, energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. Given the potential for unpaved sections, consider your tire choice.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camino de Santiago del Sur, Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, and Vereda del Camino Viejo a Fuente de Cantos.
